hypothesis: Replacing Jina Reader with Firecrawl for post-rank web context improves Skills brief quality (shadow rubric: specificity, accuracy, timely hooks) while keeping HN enrichment and 7-day momentum ranking unchanged.
change_summary: Keep DIGEST_ENRICH_WEB=1 on Skills; swap web fetch provider from Jina Reader (default) to Firecrawl via DIGEST_ENRICH_WEB_PROVIDER=firecrawl (requires FIRECRAWL_API_KEY).
notes: Baseline started 2026-07-18 (9 days early vs original 2026-07-27 plan). Control = Jina Reader + HN with DIGEST_ENRICH_WEB=1 (already on in digest.yml). Runs in parallel with EXP-20260701-landing-layout. Soft-dedup (EXP-20260715) waits until this completes. Backlog: ENRICH-2. Ship Firecrawl adapter behind DIGEST_ENRICH_WEB_PROVIDER before treatment (2026-08-01). METRIC PRIORITY (low traffic): (1) PRIMARY — shadow rubric scores (control Jina vs treatment Firecrawl) and qualitative brief quality. (2) SECONDARY — PostHog outbound_repo_click / pageviews; collect but do not gate. Pre-treatment: local shadow run with provider=firecrawl and DIGEST_ENRICH_SHADOW=1; score with briefings/lab/shadow-rubric.md. Secrets: FIRECRAWL_API_KEY in GHA. Import snapshots via npm run experiment -- snapshot after each window.
